A bunch of them are not galaxy_codex issues but [usegalaxy-au/galaxy-labs-engine](https://github.com/usegalaxy-au/galaxy-labs-engine) issues. I propose an issue template like:

> **Do you have a Labs Issue?**
> - If your issue relates to features of the Labs Engine, please post an issue [here](https://github.com/usegalaxy-au/galaxy-labs-engine/issues) instead. Example: "Can I do X in a Galaxy Lab?", "The Labs documentation does not mention X", "I got this error on my Lab page".
> - If your issue relates to the content of a Lab, please proceed to post an issue here. someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

This PR provides an example: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/6

someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

This PR provides an example: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/6

someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

This PR provides an example: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/6

someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

This PR provides an example: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/6

**EDIT: this workflow requires a scoped github token to be set as a repository secret `GH_PR_TOKEN`.**

I also realised that posting PR comments in this workflow would not work on PRs from a fork, because of how `${{ github.token }}` is scoped in pull request events. The only way I could figure out how to make this work was by using a scoped token of my own, which has read/write access to pull requests on the Codex repo. I tested these changes in my fork here: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/7

But since I do not have sufficient permissions to create an action secret (or a scoped token) for GalaxyProject/galaxy_codex, I would require someone with those permissions to do this. It goes something like this:

**Create token**
- Go to GitHub settings > Developer options > Personal Access Tokens > Fine-grained tokens > Generate
  - Expiration: Never (it's such a narrow scope I think this is ok)
  - Repo access: Select repositories 
  - Repo permissions: GalaxyProject/galaxy_codex > pull requests > read/write
  - Generate token

**Create secret**
- Go to [GalaxyProject/galaxy_codex Settings](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/settings)
- Secrets and variables > Actions

someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

This PR provides an example: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/6

**EDIT: this workflow requires a scoped github token to be set as a repository secret `GH_PR_TOKEN`.**

someone makes a PR with changes to a lab, a comment will be posted in the PR providing links to preview the lab page(s) for which changes have been made. This allows both the actor and reviewer to view the rendered webpage before merging. This is especially useful for **ensuring there are no fatal errors** before merging.

The `github-actions` comment will also test HTTP status for each version of the Lab page, and show the result in the comment. The test is re-run and the comment updated with every push, so you can see if a commit breaks or fixes a Lab page.

This PR provides an example: https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy_codex/pull/6

**EDIT: this workflow requires a scoped github token to be set as a repository secret `GH_PR_TOKEN`.**

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19054","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19054/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19054/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19054/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19054","id":2611541425,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc5_xXY3","number":19054,"title":"Implement tool markdown reports.","user":{"login":"jmchilton","id":216771,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIxNjc3MQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/216771?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton","html_url":"https://github.com/jmchilton","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jmchilton/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":180024671,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODAwMjQ2NzE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/enhancement","name":"kind/enhancement","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":317005824,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMTcwMDU4MjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/tool-framework","name":"area/tool-framework","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-24T13:05:16Z","updated_at":"2024-10-27T20:46:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/19054","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19054","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19054.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19054.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Many tools produce HTML and we do our best to support those but they are less than ideal. We cannot safely trust those HTML files to be rendered after they have been imported - probably even rendering the HTML of "safe tools" will catch up with us at some point. The ability to be able to safely render these Markdown components even after an export and re-import makes them more reproducible IMO. Once implemented, it will be much easier to compose these chunks and embed them into workflow reports also and even in the PDF version of those reports - where we couldn't really do much with HTML at all I believe.

I've included two test tools. One that links to and display the outputs of the tool and a second one that utilizes "extra files" content of the report itself - if the tool would like to included data in the report that shouldn't be a formal output of the tool. I've discovered some limitations around extra files rendering and I've included some bug fixes to get the existing components to work. There is still work to be done around these components but I think that is independent of this task.

The next steps include documenting which directives are available to tools (some don't make sense like workflow_license), better reporting when there are errors, and allowing the embed of these outputs into workflow and page reports after the fact.

xref https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19039

![Screenshot 2024-10-24 at 1 19 08 PM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63c7020d-2c46-451c-835d-47fd0a58a3cc)

+---
+title: 'Using Large Language Models in complex workflows'
+date: '2024-09-02'
+tease: "Use ChatGPT in your analysis on the European Galaxy Server to leverage the Large Language Model in your automated workflows"
+hide_tease: false
+authors: Arash Kadkhodaei, Daniela Schneider 
+subsites: [global, eu, us, freiburg]
+tags: [tool]
+---
+
+## Using Large Language Models in complex workflows
+
+The amount of data researchers face is growing daily. Finding ways of tackling the information overflow to get and analyse relevant data effectively is more critical than ever. Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT can help in this endeavour and (with good prompting) analyse text data flexibly and effectively. 
+To help do so, GPT is now available on [Galaxy](https://usegalaxy.eu/), an open-source platform for FAIR data analysis. This enables users to incorporate the LLM into more complex, automatable workflows and analyses. Galaxy Europe contains over 3,500 tools that users can combine in workflows to cover digital analysis without programming skills or expensive hardware. Users can share all steps and results of the analysis according to the FAIR principles, facilitating high reproducibility, transparency and efficient research data management.
+
+
+## Setting up GPT
+
+To access GPT in Galaxy, create an OpenAI account [here](https://platform.openai.com/signup). If needed, [add API credits](https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/billing) according to your usage.  Go to https://platform.openai.com/api-keys  to “+Create new secret key”, name it “Galaxy”, and click the green button “create secret key”. Copy this key to add the API into Galaxy.
+
+![OpenAI API key](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9424a44d-11e3-4594-bca9-d759c67956bd)
+
+
+Back in Galaxy, click “User” and then click on “Manage Information”. In the opened window, scroll down to “Your OpenAI API key”. Paste the API key you created on [OpenAI](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys) here and click “save”. 
+
+
+## Using GPT in Galaxy
+
+Now you are set to use ChatGPT in Galaxy and can choose from different models: 
+
+| Model  | Description |
+| ------------- | ------------- |
+| GPT-4o  | For complex, multi-step tasks, with vision capabilities |
+| GPT-4o mini  | Affordable and intelligent small model for fast, lightweight tasks, with vision capabilities |
+| GPT-4 Turbo and GPT-4 | The previous set of high-intelligence models, Turbo with vision capabilities |
+| GPT-3.5 Turbo | A fast, inexpensive model for simple tasks |
+
+You can also combine the ChatGPT tool in Galaxy with the other tools within a more complex workflow:
+
+You could, for example, upload audio and video files from various sources to be automatically converted into text in Galaxy using [Whisper](https://usegalaxy.eu/?tool_id=whisper). After a short cleaning, ChatGPT can summarise the text, as visualised below.
+
+![ChatGPT tool](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd1a82d5-be79-464f-8f57-9fe5487e2abf)
+
+The screenshot shows the integration of ChatGPT as a tool in Galaxy. The penal on the right side, the ‘History’, shows an example workflow. Here, a mp3-recording from Martin Luther King’s ‘I have a dream’ was converted into text, cleaned, and translated using ChatGPT. The prompt was adapted from [Prompting ChatGPT for {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164","id":2468929840,"node_id":"I_kwDOIQitUM6TKOEw","number":164,"title":"Restructure codex for community content","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-08-15T20:52:15Z","updated_at":"2024-09-04T23:25:36Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"As discussed with @bebatut and @nomadscientist. The repo seems to be laid out in a way that is friendly for developers, but not so much for community (potential non-coding) users.

It would be great if we could do a restructure of the repo to improve this. Since it is supposed to be a place for community members to access metadata, I suggest something like:

```
bin/
    *.sh
    *.py
    README.md
communities/
    microgalaxy/  # If I'm a MicroGalaxy user, everything I need is in here
        lab/
        metadata/
    singlecell/
        [...]
    [...]
README.md
```

Where:

1. The current README.md is moved to ./bin/README.md.
2. A new README, aimed at community users, is written at ./README.md, as suggested in #147.
3. Results from metadata extractor are written to `communities/<name>/metadata/`.
4. Lab (aka subdomain) content is written to `communities/<name>/lab/`.

I don't mind doing some of the work here, for now I'm just hoping for a bit of discussion on this. ### Overview

This PR introduces the "subdomains" folder to codex for globally-centralized Galaxy Lab webpage content. 

Community members will then be able to make changes to their lab page through pull requests to this repo.
Lab pages can be requested by a rendering engine hosted at https://site.usegalaxy.org.au/lab/export. The rendering engine will soon be deployed as a standalone service.

> [!WARNING]
> The files under `subdomains/singlecell/` will be used to render production services (Galaxy Labs).
> Changes to these file paths will break production services. These paths should never be changed without
> updating consumers (e.g. usegalaxy.org, usegalaxy.eu, usegalaxy.org.au)

### Summary of changes

- `subdomains/README.md` documents process for creating a new Galaxy Lab
- `subdomains/scripts/` for processing tool installation metadata
- Add `singlecell` lab at `subdomains/singlecell/` (currently deployed on https://singlecell.usegalaxy.org.au)

### Future direction

Once the repo has been re-organised a bit, we will refactor subdomains as subfolders in the relevant community folder. 

e.g:

```
single-cell/
├── lab/
└── other_stuff/
microgalaxy/
├── lab/
└── other_stuff/

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164","id":2468929840,"node_id":"I_kwDOIQitUM6TKOEw","number":164,"title":"Restructure codex for community content","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-15T20:52:15Z","updated_at":"2024-08-15T20:52:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"As discussed with @bebatut and @nomadscientist. The repo seems to be laid out in a way that is friendly for developers, but not so much for community (potential non-coding) users.

It would be great if we could do a restructure of the repo to improve this. Since it is supposed to be a place for community members to access metadata, I suggest something like:

```
bin/
    *.sh
    *.py
    README.md
communities/
    microgalaxy/  # If I'm a MicroGalaxy user, everything I need is in here
        lab/
        metadata/
    singlecell/
        [...]
    [...]
README.md
```

Where:

1. The current README.md is moved to ./bin/README.md.
2. A new README, aimed at community users, is written at ./README.md, as suggested in #147.
3. Results from metadata extractor are written to `communities/<name>/metadata/`.
4. Lab (aka subdomain) content is written to `communities/<name>/lab/`.

I don't mind doing some of the work here, for now I'm just hoping for a bit of discussion on this. Thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy_codex/issues/164/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

to "per-host configuration" of Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/12328

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.2.1.dev0 https://dev.gvl.org.au/
Commit: 8bda304592e448510470ba94594ee64357d969c4

I am trying to set the `default_panel_view` as a `by_host` parameter. However, when I set the following in galaxy.yml on [Galaxy AU Dev](https://dev.gvl.org.au) it seems to have no effect. The tool panel loads the normal default:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au: proteomics
```

However, when I try to replicate the bug locally, the following seems to work fine i.e. I get `proteomics` as the default panel when I open the site on http://localhost/:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      localhost: proteomics
```

So it seems like this feature works, but for some hostnames it stops working?

**Additional context**
I can confirm the same pattern with `support_url_by_host` (works locally, not on dev.gvl.org.au), so this seems to be a general issue with the `_by_host` feature and not limited to the `default_panel_view` param:

WORKS: `default_tool_panel: proteomics`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
WORKS (local): `support_url_by_host: localhost/127.0.0.1`

021-08-25T16:01:07Z"},"body":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/12327 implements nifty tool panel views that are generalizations of the toolbox filters used by *.usegalaxy.eu (e.g. https://rna.usegalaxy.eu or https://covid19.usegalaxy.eu). To generalize that experience of different panel defaults depending on the subdomain Galaxy is hosted on though I want to be able to swap the new parameter `default_panel_view` on a per-domain basis.

This PR implements that pretty easily by simply annotating a variety of parameters as `per_host: true` in the configuration schema. Galaxy will then accept `<config_param>_by_host` variants of the parameters annotated this that accept dicts of the form ``{host: val_for_host}``. This should make it possible to serve a bunch of flavored Galaxies from a single set of server process with slightly different client configurations.

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Update galaxy.yml to include:
  ```
  support_url_by_host:
    'localhost': https://twitter.com/
    '127.0.0.1': https://google.com/
   ```
  2. Start Galaxy and navigate to both localhost:8080 and 127.0.0.1:8080 and notice how the support url in the Help menu changes between Twitter and Google.

... but this will:

to "per-host configuration" of Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/12328

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.2.1.dev0 https://dev.gvl.org.au/
Commit: 8bda304592e448510470ba94594ee64357d969c4

I am trying to set the `default_panel_view` as a `by_host` parameter. However, when I set the following in galaxy.yml on [Galaxy AU Dev](https://dev.gvl.org.au) it seems to have no effect. The tool panel loads the normal default:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au: proteomics
```

However, when I try to replicate the bug locally, the following seems to work fine i.e. I get `proteomics` as the default panel when I open the site on http://localhost/:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      localhost: proteomics
```

So it seems like this feature works, but for some hostnames it stops working?

**Additional context**
I can confirm the same pattern with `support_url_by_host` (works locally, not on dev.gvl.org.au), so this seems to be a general issue with the `_by_host` feature and not limited to the `default_panel_view` param:

WORKS: `default_tool_panel: proteomics`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
WORKS (local): `support_url_by_host: localhost/127.0.0.1`

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.2.1.dev0 https://dev.gvl.org.au/
Commit: 8bda304592e448510470ba94594ee64357d969c4

I am trying to set the `default_panel_view` as a `by_host` parameter. However, when I set the following in galaxy.yml on [Galaxy AU Dev](https://dev.gvl.org.au) it seems to have no effect. The tool panel loads the normal default:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au: proteomics
```

However, when I try to replicate the bug locally, the following seems to work fine i.e. I get `proteomics` as the default panel when I open the site on http://localhost/:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      localhost: proteomics
```

So it seems like this feature works, but for some hostnames it stops working?

**Additional context**
I can confirm the same pattern with `support_url_by_host` (works locally, not on dev.gvl.org.au), so this seems to be a general issue with the `_by_host` feature and not limited to the `default_panel_view` param:

WORKS: `default_tool_panel: proteomics`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
WORKS (local): `support_url_by_host: localhost/127.0.0.1`

```py
            for host_key, host_val in per_host.items():
                if per_host_option == 'support_url_by_host':
                    log.info(f"LOADING SUPPORT URL | host: {host}, host_key: {host_key}, host_val: {host_val}")
```

With the following galaxy.yml config:

```yml
support_url_by_host:
  'dev.gvl.org.au': https://site.usegalaxy.org.au/help
  'proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au': https://google.com 
```

And on dev.gvl.org.au this produces the following logs on request of proteomics.gvl.org.au:

```
LOADING SUPPORT URL | host: proteomics.gvl.org.au, host_key: dev.gvl.org.au, host_val: https://site.usegalaxy.org.au/help
```

Now, looking at the code in config/__init__.py, it obviously matches the first key that is a substring of `host`. 

So, when using this feature for subdomains, this won't work: 
```yml
support_url_by_host:
  'dev.gvl.org.au': https://site.usegalaxy.org.au/help
  'proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au': https://google.com 
```

... but this will:
```yml
support_url_by_host:
  'proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au': https://google.com
  'dev.gvl.org.au': https://site.usegalaxy.org.au/help 
```

I will link this from the PR so that others can see this issue. @jdavcs I'll leave it up to you whether this is a bug or a feature!

to "per-host configuration" of Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/12328

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.2.1.dev0 https://dev.gvl.org.au/
Commit: 8bda304592e448510470ba94594ee64357d969c4

I am trying to set the `default_panel_view` as a `by_host` parameter. However, when I set the following in galaxy.yml on [Galaxy AU Dev](https://dev.gvl.org.au) it seems to have no effect. The tool panel loads the normal default:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au: proteomics
```

However, when I try to replicate the bug locally, the following seems to work fine i.e. I get `proteomics` as the default panel when I open the site on http://localhost/:

```
    default_panel_view_by_host:
      localhost: proteomics
```

So it seems like this feature works, but for some hostnames it stops working?

**Additional context**
I can confirm the same pattern with `support_url_by_host` (works locally, not on dev.gvl.org.au), so this seems to be a general issue with the `_by_host` feature and not limited to the `default_panel_view` param:

WORKS: `default_tool_panel: proteomics`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `default_tool_panel_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: dev.gvl.org.au`
NOT WORKING: `support_url_by_host: proteomics.dev.gvl.org.au`
WORKS (local): `support_url_by_host: localhost/127.0.0.1`

CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] This PR adds a new tool or tool collection
* [ ] This PR updates an existing tool or tool collection
* [x] This PR does something else (explain below)
---

This tool is causing a bit of support work when users input nucleotide sequences but select protein as the sequence type.

This greps for anything that is not an IUPAC nucleotide and checks the result against the selected seqtype.

It won't always be correct because a protein consisting only of amino acid residues that are in the nucleotide alphabet is valid (but probably not common). It could also take a long time to search through valid nucleotide input. I could limit it to the first 1000 lines or something?

Ping @igormakunin

CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] This PR adds a new tool or tool collection
* [ ] This PR updates an existing tool or tool collection
* [x] This PR does something else (explain below)
---

This tool is causing a bit of support work when users input nucleotide sequences but select protein as the sequence type.

This greps for anything that is not an IUPAC nucleotide and checks the result against the selected seqtype.

It won't always be correct because a protein consisting only of amino acid residues that are in the nucleotide alphabet is valid (but probably not common). It could also take a long time to search through valid nucleotide input. I could limit it to the first 1000 lines or something?

Ping @igormakunin

CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)

---

This tool is causing a bit of support work when users input nucleotide sequences but select protein as the sequence type.

This greps for anything that is not an IUPAC nucleotide and checks the result against the selected seqtype.

It won't always be correct because a protein consisting only of amino acid residues that are in the nucleotide alphabet is valid (but probably not common). It could also take a long time to search through valid nucleotide input. I could limit it to the first 1000 lines or something?

Ping @igormakunin

Ages ago I did start developing a Python lib `fastkit` for that reason: https://github.com/neoformit/fastkit

It has a validator for DNA, protein, sequence count etc. and some common formatting for FASTA files. Could also implement for FASTQ but haven't yet. It raises stderr that results in user-friendly error messages in Galaxy.

Maybe there are existing libraries but I don't know of one that does this exactly. This tool is causing a bit of support work when users input nucleotide sequences but select protein as the sequence type.

This greps for anything that is not an IUPAC nucleotide and checks the result against the selected seqtype.

It won't always be correct because a protein consisting only of amino acid residues that are in the nucleotide alphabet is valid (but probably not common). It could also take a long time to search through valid nucleotide input. I could limit it to the first 1000 lines or something?

Ping @igormakunin

`util.send_mail` should accept `reply_to` as an optional kwarg:
https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/0d03c1bc4a5d89ee8323364b0ddd917fbfd2fbea/lib/galaxy/util/__init__.py#L1542

And then the error handler can pass the appropriate `reply_to` value to `util.send_mail`:
https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/0d03c1bc4a5d89ee8323364b0ddd917fbfd2fbea/lib/galaxy/tools/errors.py#L257-L259

I would suggest making this configurable in `galaxy.yml` with something like `error_reply_to_user: Summary: can outputs be renamed differently? 

Running on Galaxy Australia, Trimmomatic version 0.36.6

Inputs: Paired-end reads: 2 collections (one of R1 files, one of R2 files)

Output required: Trimmed reads, and a collection of log files (not trimlog files). 

(Edited to update with a more general problem with output file naming, not only relevant to output log files)

Issue: when input files are named in the format `sample123_R1.fastqsanger` and `sample123_R2.fastqsanger`, output file names all take on the former name only, e.g. `sample123_R1.fastqsanger`

I think it would be better if outputs (where they refer to both R1 and R2 files) could be labelled without the R1 extension, such as `sample123.fastqsanger`

This would be clearer for the user, and also for downstream tools (such as plots in MultiQC, which are thus also labelled as being R1 files, when they are actually relating to both R1 and R2 files). Summary: can outputs be renamed differently? 

Running on Galaxy Australia, Trimmomatic version 0.36.6

Inputs: Paired-end reads: 2 collections (one of R1 files, one of R2 files)

Output required: Trimmed reads, and a collection of log files (not trimlog files). 

(Edited to update with a more general problem with output file naming, not only relevant to output log files)

Issue: when input files are named in the format `sample123_R1.fastqsanger` and `sample123_R2.fastqsanger`, output file names all take on the former name only, e.g. `sample123_R1.fastqsanger`

I think it would be better if outputs (where they refer to both R1 and R2 files) could be labelled without the R1 extension, such as `sample123.fastqsanger`

This would be clearer for the user, and also for downstream tools (such as plots in MultiQC, which are thus also labelled as being R1 files, when they are actually relating to both R1 and R2 files). It seems more like a Galaxy Collections bug than tool wrapper issue:

![image](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/assets/42562517/0246723d-c231-48c0-9a3d-2b9f7e61d2ce)

1. The Trimmomatic job was submitted with paired reads in two collections: R1 and R2
2. The job outputs four collections: Unpaired R1, Unpaired R2, Paired R1, Paired R2
3. In the screenshot above the "Unpaired R2" collection is selected in the History
4. Note the input files `gah.fastqsanger` (R1) and `GSM461181_2.fastqsanger` (R2)
5. Now look at the first element in the History. It has been named after the first input dataset in the job (R1), and not after `${readtype.fastq_r2_in.name}` (highlighted in Dataset info panel) as I would have expected.
6. The result is that the `XXX_R2.fastqsanger` files are being named `XXX_R1.fastqsanger` which looks like a bug?

@bernt-matthias can you make any sense of this? Does Galaxy indeed infer collection element names from input dataset names? Does the tool developer have any control over this?
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/comments/1897774786/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Running on Galaxy Australia, Trimmomatic version 0.36.6

Inputs: Paired-end reads: 2 collections (one of R1 files, one of R2 files)

Output required: Trimmed reads, and a collection of log files (not trimlog files). 

(Edited to update with a more general problem with output file naming, not only relevant to output log files)

Issue: when input files are named in the format `sample123_R1.fastqsanger` and `sample123_R2.fastqsanger`, output file names all take on the former name only, e.g. `sample123_R1.fastqsanger`

I think it would be better if outputs (where they refer to both R1 and R2 files) could be labelled without the R1 extension, such as `sample123.fastqsanger`

This would be clearer for the user, and also for downstream tools (such as plots in MultiQC, which are thus also labelled as being R1 files, when they are actually relating to both R1 and R2 files). ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/comments/1905051486","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703#issuecomment-1905051486","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703","id":1905051486,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAW4Y0s5xjMde","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-23T00:03:17Z","updated_at":"2024-01-23T00:03:17Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Tested on Galaxy AU dev (dev.gvl.org.au)

`default_identifier_source` fixes the output collection element IDs for R1/R1 outputs:

```xml
        <data name="fastq_out_r2_paired" default_identifier_source="readtype|fastq_r2_in" label="${tool.name} on ${readtype.fastq_r2_in.name} (R2 paired)" format_source="fastq_r2_in">
            <filter>
                readtype['single_or_paired'] == "pair_of_files"
            </filter>
        </data>
```

![image](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/assets/42562517/9f98b749-fd9e-4624-90f1-b38c81dd7ad0)

But I don't think there is a good way to name neutral output elements, like log messages? They should be like `GSM461181.fastqsanger`. This probably isn't syntactically correct but something like this might be a compromise?

```xml
default_identifier_source="readtype|fastq_r1 + readtype|fastq_r2"
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729","id":2097129884,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s58_6mc","number":5729,"title":"Infer collection element naming with `default_identifier_source`","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-01-23T23:03:16Z","updated_at":"2024-01-23T23:03:16Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When collections are created as a tool output, the Galaxy tool author can declare output element names by referencing input datasets. However, this feature is poorly documented and seems to be used in only one IUC tool. Its use is relevant for any tool that accepts paired inputs and produces paired outputs. Without this declaration, Galaxy will default to naming output collection elements after the first input dataset element. This results in "R2" outputs being named as "R1" which is obviously misleading and confusing for the user. 

As described in issue https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703:

![image](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/assets/42562517/0246723d-c231-48c0-9a3d-2b9f7e61d2ce)

1. The Trimmomatic job was submitted with paired reads in two collections: R1 and R2
2. The job outputs four collections: Unpaired R1, Unpaired R2, Paired R1, Paired R2
3. In the screenshot above the "Unpaired R2" collection is selected in the History
4. Note the input files `gah.fastqsanger` (R1) and `GSM461181_2.fastqsanger` (R2)
5. Now look at the first element in the History. It has been named after the first input dataset in the job (R1), and not after `${readtype.fastq_r2_in.name}` (highlighted in Dataset info panel) as I would have expected.
6. The result is that the `XXX_R2.fastqsanger` files are being named `XXX_R1.fastqsanger` which looks like a bug?

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5729","id":2097129884,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s58_6mc","number":5729,"title":"Infer collection element naming with `default_identifier_source`","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-01-23T23:03:16Z","updated_at":"2024-01-25T04:21:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"# The issue 
When collections are created as a tool output, the Galaxy tool author can declare output element names by referencing input datasets. However, this feature is poorly documented and seems to be used in only one IUC tool, [macs2_callpeak](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/main/tools/macs2/macs2_callpeak.xml). Its use is relevant for any tool that accepts paired inputs and produces paired outputs (i.e. many tools, including but not limited to IUC). Without this declaration, Galaxy will default to naming output collection elements after the first input dataset element. This results in "R2" outputs being named as "R1" which is obviously misleading and confusing for the user.

Part of this issue is that the `default_identifier_source` attribute doesn't seem to be documented properly in the [Tool XML Schema](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/schema.html#id78) page. In particular, the weird pipe syntax for referencing nested variables (e.g. `conditional_name|param_name`).

<hr>

As described in issue https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703:

![image](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/assets/42562517/0246723d-c231-48c0-9a3d-2b9f7e61d2ce)

1. The Trimmomatic job was submitted with paired reads in two collections: R1 and R2
2. The job outputs four collections: Unpaired R1, Unpaired R2, Paired R1, Paired R2
3. In the screenshot above the "Unpaired R2" collection is selected in the History
4. Note the input files `gah.fastqsanger` (R1) and `GSM461181_2.fastqsanger` (R2)
5. Now look at the first element in the History. It has been named after the first input dataset in the job (R1), and not after `${readtype.fastq_r2_in.name}` (highlighted in Dataset info panel) as I would have expected.
6. The result is that the `XXX_R2.fastqsanger` files are being named `XXX_R1.fastqsanger` which looks like a bug?

The [above issue](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5703) refers to [Trimmomatic](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/7568e92bfc4560d6e0287e7444455f55c86d135d/tools/trimmomatic/trimmomatic.xml#L225-L227), which has been fixed by https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pull/5728.

# Proposed solution

- A repo-wide application of the `default_identifier_source` attribute on `<data>` tags, where appropriate, as has been applied to [Trimmomatic](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/7568e92bfc4560d6e0287e7444455f55c86d135d/tools/trimmomatic/trimmomatic.xml#L225-L227):
    ```xml
    <data name="fastq_out_r1_paired" default_identifier_source="readtype|fastq_r1_in" label="${tool.name} on 
    ${readtype.fastq_r1_in.name} (R1 paired)" format_source="fastq_r1_in">
    ```
- Proper documentation of the `default_identifier_source` attribute in the [Tool XML Schema](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/schema.html#id78)

Right... the pipe is rendered as a table column. It would be nice if that could be fixed so the documentation makes sense.

> Element identifiers are supposed to represent samples

Of course this makes sense, but users will often upload read files to collections with an `R1`/`R2` suffix. Hence why this became an issue for Anna. So we should aim to make the software intuitive for this use case, rather than expecting them to understand this problem and re-name all their collection elements.

@bernt-matthias would these tools need to be manually tested, or just apply the `default_element_identifier` and push? It's not such a long list.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/comments/1909326801/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

In the admin panel "Manage groups" view, the typed select component becomes clunky once 10+ users have been added to a group. The select dropdown is cluttered with _selected_ options, which is redundant because these options can already be removed using the main component. Furthermore, the current behaviour is that the select options close and the typed input is reset when the user clicks on an option. This is really annoying when the intention is to add multiple users with an email pattern e.g. `@myuni.edu`.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
v23.1 / Galaxy AU

**To Reproduce**
Go to admin panel > manage groups and add 10+ users to a group. See how the typed select becomes unusable.

**Expected behavior**
1. The typed select input should not show selected options, because they can already be removed from the "selected" list
2. The typed select input should not clear/disappear when an option is clicked, because this makes adding multiple users very tedious 

**Screenshots**
![image](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/42562517/1545269c-7d6a-4829-b182-2d7287fa9aa5)

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437","id":1817705625,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5sV_yZ","number":16437,"title":"Feature request - include "datatype" as a tool search field","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-07-24T06:08:12Z","updated_at":"2023-07-24T06:08:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Feature request**
It would be really nice if we could include tool input datatypes as a search field in the new advanced tool search.

Initially this could just be a typed select field for selecting valid galaxy datatypes. Only tools which specify those datatypes as inputs (and, optionally, also outputs? "I want to see tools that produce XXX data"?) should be returned. Even better would be a typed search which indexes the [dataypes_conf.xml](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/config/sample/datatypes_conf.xml.sample#L3-L8) descriptions:

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/c5b499f893d2d82c77c663644fcd44bb01419220/lib/galaxy/config/sample/datatypes_conf.xml.sample#L3-L8

This would be really helpful as Galaxy expands further into niche datatypes (e.g. proteomics). If a user wants to analyse their `wiff` data, it would be great if we could show them the tools that operate on this data. I think this would provide a much friendlier experience for new Galaxy users.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16437/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16453","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16453/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16453/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16453/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16453","id":1819240756,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5sb2k0","number":16453,"title":"Enhancement: EDAM ontology tools view improvements","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-07-24T22:29:57Z","updated_at":"2023-07-24T22:29:57Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"With @hechth @bgruening 
The EDAM ontology tools view is a great concept but would be much more useful if the following were addressed:

1. Text-searchable ontologies. Currently I have to scroll through a long list until I see an ontology that appeals to my analysis
1. Tree-view for ontologies. Why are we displaying hierarchically ordered data in flat lists?  
1. Better ontology annotation coverage across tool wrappers (outside the scope of this issue)
1. More comprehensive ontologies, particularly in emerging fields such as proteomics and metabolomics (also outside the scope of this issue).

I think 2) should be solved by opening an ontology tree in the main panel, as the new advanced search does. This could include some filters or a search box to find relevant ontologies quickly.

A common complaint of Galaxy users is that the service is bloated with tools. @hechth @bgruening 
The EDAM ontology tools view is a great concept but would be much more useful if the following were addressed:

1. Text-searchable ontologies. Currently I have to scroll through a long list until I see an ontology that appeals to my analysis
1. Tree-view for ontologies. Why are we displaying hierarchically ordered data in flat lists?  
1. Better ontology annotation coverage across tool wrappers (outside the scope of this issue)
1. More comprehensive ontologies, particularly in emerging fields such as proteomics and metabolomics (also outside the scope of this issue).

I think 2) should be solved by opening an ontology tree in the main panel, as the new advanced search does. This could include some filters or a search box to find relevant ontologies quickly.

A common complaint of Galaxy users is that the service is bloated with tools. I think features like this are critical to making the service navigable and approachable for users.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344","id":1743201963,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s5n5yar","number":5344,"title":"GTDB-Tk output files missing","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-06-06T06:50:48Z","updated_at":"2023-06-06T06:50:48Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I am reviewing a ticket from a user who is missing output files from their GTDB-Tk jobs.
On closer inspection it seems that the wrapper might not be picking up some of the output files, due to an error in the `discover_datasets` declaration. To debug this, I have run `gtdb classify_wf` manually on the CLI and observed the outputs produced by the same run. The user reported that the `.tree` and `.fasta` outputs were missing, but I can see them in the output directory of my local run. It seems that the `output_dir` of `discover_datasets` is incorrect, leading to silent failure and empty collection outputs with `success` status.

Outputs declared in the wrapper:

https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/f173f58bc695c22364685c2ffbb9f3e95708f00f/tools/gtdbtk/gtdbtk_classify_wf.xml#L52-L65

`output_dir` as seen on the server: 

```
.
├── align
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.filtered.tsv
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.msa.fasta.gz
│   └── gtdbtk.bac120.user_msa.fasta.gz
├── classify
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.classify.tree.1.tree
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.classify.tree.3.tree
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.classify.tree.4.tree
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.classify.tree.6.tree
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.classify.tree.7.tree
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.summary.tsv
│   ├── gtdbtk.bac120.tree.mapping.tsv
│   └── gtdbtk.backbone.bac120.classify.tree
├── gtdbtk.bac120.summary.tsv -> classify/gtdbtk.bac120.summary.tsv
├── gtdbtk.json
├── gtdbtk.log
├── gtdbtk.warnings.log
└── identify
    ├── gtdbtk.ar53.markers_summary.tsv
    ├── gtdbtk.bac120.markers_summary.tsv
    ├── gtdbtk.failed_genomes.tsv
    └── gtdbtk.translation_table_summary.tsv
```

So it seems that the directory for `output_newick` and `output_fasta` are incorrect:

```xml
<!-- dir should be "output_dir/classify" -->
<discover_datasets pattern="(?P<designation>.+)\.tree" ext="newick" directory="output_dir"/>

<!-- dir should be "output_dir/align" -->
<discover_datasets pattern="(?P<designation>.+)\.fasta" ext="fasta" directory="output_dir"/>
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5344/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366","id":1766201076,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s5pRhb0","number":5366,"title":"FASTQ to FASTA tool: gzipped output has fasta datatype","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-06-20T21:17:38Z","updated_at":"2023-06-20T21:17:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Just popped this out of https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5333.

This is for the fastx_toolkit tool: https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/tree/main/tool_collections/fastx_toolkit/fastq_to_fasta

**Issue:** The tool attempts to infer the FASTA output compression (none, gz or bz) from the input datatype. The tool does compress the data correctly, but always sets the output datatype as `fasta` resulting in a broken/weird dataset.

I like the suggestion from @bernt-matthias :+1: 
> My preference would be to have zipping/unzipping controlled by a select with the following options:
>
> - same as input (default)
> - zipped
> - unzipped
>
> So we have the best of both worlds. Unfortunately this brings a bit of "heavy" logic for determining the output format. Could be implemented with tool provided metadata (galaxy.json).
> 
> Otherwise my (weak) preference would be "same as input"...
> 
> What about fastqsanger.bz
> 
> Is this a thing? :)

... so will implement that.

I'm working on a solution for inferring the output datatype that uses two outputs (one `fasta`, one `fasta.gz`) with a filter on each. Something like:

```xml
        <data name="output" format="fasta" metadata_source="input">
            <filter>
                not param_compressed_output
                or input.is_of_type('fasta', 'fastqsanger', 'fastqsolexa', 'fastqillumina')
            </filter>
        </data>
        <data name="output" format="fasta.gz" metadata_source="input">
            <filter> param_compressed_output
                or input.is_of_type(
                    'fasta.gz',
                    'fastqsanger.gz',
                    'fastqsolexa.gz',
                    'fastqillumina.gz',
                    'fastqsanger.bz2',
                    'fastqsolexa.bz2',
                    'fastqillumina.bz2',
                )
            </filter>
        </data>
```

Looks somewhat hacky to me, but it's the only way I could see to make the `format` attribute dynamic (unless there is some magic to be done with metadata as Matthias suggests). At least it's fairly readable.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366","id":1766201076,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s5pRhb0","number":5366,"title":"FASTQ to FASTA tool: gzipped output has fasta datatype","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-06-20T21:17:38Z","updated_at":"2023-06-23T09:39:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Just popped this out of https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5333.

This is for the fastx_toolkit tool: https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/tree/main/tool_collections/fastx_toolkit/fastq_to_fasta

**Issue:** The tool attempts to infer the FASTA output compression (none, gz or bz) from the input datatype. The tool does compress the data correctly, but always sets the output datatype as `fasta` resulting in a broken/weird dataset.

I like the suggestion from @bernt-matthias :+1: 
> My preference would be to have zipping/unzipping controlled by a select with the following options:
>
> - same as input (default)
> - zipped
> - unzipped
>
> So we have the best of both worlds. Unfortunately this brings a bit of "heavy" logic for determining the output format. Could be implemented with tool provided metadata (galaxy.json).
> 
> Otherwise my (weak) preference would be "same as input"...
> 
> What about fastqsanger.bz
> 
> Is this a thing? :)

... so will implement that.

I'm working on a solution for inferring the output datatype that uses two outputs (one `fasta`, one `fasta.gz`) with a filter on each. Something like:

```xml
        <data name="output" format="fasta" metadata_source="input">
            <filter>
                compress_output == 'no'
                or compress_output == 'infer'
                and input.is_of_type('fasta', 'fastqsanger', 'fastqsolexa', 'fastqillumina')
            </filter>
        </data>
        <data name="output" format="fasta.gz" metadata_source="input">
            <filter>
                compress_output == 'yes'
                or compress_output == 'infer'
                and input.is_of_type(
                    'fasta.gz',
                    'fastqsanger.gz',
                    'fastqsolexa.gz',
                    'fastqillumina.gz',
                    'fastqsanger.bz2',
                    'fastqsolexa.bz2',
                    'fastqillumina.bz2',
                )
            </filter>
        </data>
```

Looks somewhat hacky to me, but it's the only way I could see to make the `format` attribute dynamic (unless there is some magic to be done with metadata as Matthias suggests). At least it's fairly readable.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/comments/1604014886","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366#issuecomment-1604014886","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5366","id":1604014886,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAW4Y0s5fm1Mm","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-23T09:39:03Z","updated_at":"2023-06-23T09:39:03Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"@bernt-matthias ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/comments/1604014886/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

this is another case where the tool infers output datatype based on the input and this now seems to fail (could it be a 23.0 bug?).

- Input: `fastqsanger`
- Output: `fastqsanger` datatype assigned to `fastqsanger.gz`

I created an additional test to catch the above issue:

```xml
<!-- paired data in paired collection with uncompressed input -->
<test expect_num_outputs="4">
    <conditional name="paired_cond">
        <param name="paired_select" value="paired"/>
        <param name="reads">
            <collection type="paired">
                <element name="forward" value="F3D0_S188_L001_R1_001.fastq" ftype="fastqsanger"/>
                <element name="reverse" value="F3D0_S188_L001_R2_001.fastq" ftype="fastqsanger"/>
            </collection>
        </param>
    </conditional>
    <output_collection name="paired_output" type="paired" count="2">
        <element name="forward" value="filterAndTrim_F3D0_R1.fq" ftype="fastqsanger" />
        <element name="reverse" value="filterAndTrim_F3D0_R2.fq" ftype="fastqsanger" />
    </output_collection>
    <output name="outtab" value="F3D0_S188_L001_R1_001.tab" ftype="tabular" lines_diff="2">
        <assert_contents>
            <has_text text="745" />
            <has_text text="750" />
        </assert_contents>
    </output>
</test>
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5377/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/5377/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

for galaxy servers using subdomains (e.g. rna.usegalaxy.eu) there is no way for users to easily switch between sites, or even see what sites are available. This PR adds an optional & configurable masthead/navbar dropdown for Galaxy subdomains/community domains site links. This masthead item is only displayed when a `subdomains_conf.xml` config file is present. Masthead items matching the current `location.origin` are hidden. 

e.g.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/233899362-5dfae703-174c-43de-a1ce-eea787050fd6.png)

for galaxy servers using subdomains (e.g. `rna.usegalaxy.eu`) there is no way for users to easily switch between sites, or even see what sites are available. This PR adds an optional & configurable masthead/navbar dropdown for Galaxy subdomains/community domains site links. This masthead item is only displayed when a `subdomains_conf.xml` config file is present. Domain links matching the current `location.origin` are hidden. 

e.g.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/233899362-5dfae703-174c-43de-a1ce-eea787050fd6.png)

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296","id":1530079426,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5bMyjC","number":15296,"title":"Archive upload UI","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-01-12T04:29:47Z","updated_at":"2023-03-02T03:41:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This is an extension to John Chilton's https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202 so we can flesh out how the archive upload/extract feature will behave.

I have made a crude start on [this branch](https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy/tree/archive-uploads). There are lots of open ends and this is a long way from PR.

## Current status
- [x] Upload single tar/zip archive
- [x] Extract flattened content to the current history

The API accepts tar/zip upload requests at `/api/tools/fetch` where `target.extract_from = 'archive'`.
Can also pass `extract_from=bagit_archive` to extract an archive (tar/zip) packaged in bagit format. See [data_fetch.py](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/data_fetch.py#L95-L98).

**Bugs**
- [ ] Reset button
- [ ] Cancel button
- [ ] Genome select in table row
- [ ] Remove "Selected 1 file(s)" while `multiple="false"`
- [ ] `auto` datatype - see below

**Things that we probably want to do**
- [ ] Handle URL fetch
- [ ] Set datatype explicitly (useful for URL fetch when filename is nonsense)
- [ ] Handle yaml description (I'm not even sure what this means, but [John mentioned it](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202))
- [ ] Handle zip archives with RO-crate format (depends on client-side RO-crate libs)
- [ ] Allow upload to a collection (rather than dumping into history)
- [ ] Checkbox to upload into a new history
- [ ] Create an archive wizard flow, where user can explore the archive contents and selectively upload (see below)
- [ ] Accept history dataset (e.g. `tar`) as an input (there's way to extract an archive in the history currently)

**Things that we might *not* want to do**
- We don't want to upload a whole archive unless really necessary. As much client-side as possible.
- Upload multiple archives: not sure this makes sense with the "extract wizard" flow unless we use a rule-builder approach

## Features

**Datatype select**
Each datatype can be handled in multiple ways (e.g. zip can be either bagit/RO-crate spec). We probably want a `format` select component with options `None`, `BagIt`, `RO-crate` that could apply to either a zip/tar upload.

**Extract wizard**
- Blindly dumping an entire archive into the current history will often create a horrible UX
- Assume that the user might want to extract archive elements to multiple collections/histories
- Either examine and display archive content in the client, or have the backend return a dir tree that the user can explore in the frontend (the latter is probably required for URL fetch)
- The user can then checkbox-select files/dirs and choose to send them to a history as datasets or collection
- As above, but use the rule-builder
