is an extension to John Chilton's https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202 so we can flesh out how the archive upload/extract feature will behave.

I have made a crude start on [this branch](https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy/tree/archive-uploads). There are lots of open ends and this is a long way from PR.

## Current status

The API accepts tar/zip upload requests at `/api/tools/fetch` where `target.extract_from = 'archive'`.
Can also pass `extract_from=bagit_archive` to extract an archive (tar/zip) packaged in bagit format. See [data_fetch.py](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/data_fetch.py#L95-L98).

However, we probably want to do the extraction in the client as much as possible (consider user wants to upload 2MB of files from 2 GB archive). Which means displaying a tree-view of the archive contents in the UI, extracting selecting datasets and uploading them with the standard dataset/collection upload API.

**Things that we probably want to do**
- [ ] Handle URL fetch (requires asyncronous download/extraction in backend)
- [ ] Set datatype explicitly (useful for URL fetch when filename is nonsense)
- [ ] Handle yaml description (I'm not even sure what this means, but [John mentioned it](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202))
- [ ] Handle zip archives in RO-crate spec (depends on client-side RO-crate libs)
- [ ] Allow upload to a collection (rather than dumping into history)
- [ ] Checkbox to upload into a new history
- [ ] Create an archive wizard flow, where user can explore the archive contents and selectively upload (see below)
- [ ] Accept history dataset (e.g. `tar`) as an input (there's way to extract an archive in the history currently)

**Things that we might *not* want to do**
- We don't want to upload a whole archive unless really necessary. As much client-side as possible.
- Upload multiple archives: not sure this makes sense with the "extract wizard" flow unless we use a rule-builder approach

## Features

**Datatype select**
Each datatype can be handled in multiple ways (e.g. zip can be either bagit/RO-crate spec). We probably want a `format` select component with options `None`, `BagIt`, `RO-crate` that could apply to either a zip/tar upload.

**Extract wizard**
- Blindly dumping an entire archive into the current history will often create a horrible UX
- Assume that the user might want to extract archive elements to multiple collections/histories
- Either examine and display archive content in the client, or have the backend return a dir tree that the user can explore in the frontend (the latter is probably required for URL fetch)
- The user can then checkbox-select files/dirs and choose to send them to a history as datasets or collection
- As above, but use the rule-builder

**URL fetch**
- This would be enabled implicitly if we allow history datasets as archive input.

A ticket from our users at usegalaxy.org.au:

> There seems to be a reproducible glitch in the Multiple Sequence Alignment viewer: attempting to slide the scale bar freezes Firefox on Ubuntu 22.04. After sliding the scale bar knob the page becomes completely unresponsive, and eventually crashes (screencast below).

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 22.05

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. View a FASTA sequence alignment with the MSA viewer 
2. Zoom in/out and see if it freezes and crashes your browser

**Expected behavior**
Should not crash

**Screenshots**

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/222986106-6b97ab26-129c-43ee-ab8c-60fb2b66f9a4.mp4


**Additional context**
Not sure if this was resolved by https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/14263.

Which means displaying a tree-view of the archive contents in the UI, extracting selecting datasets and uploading them with the standard dataset/collection upload API.

**Things that we probably want to do**
- [ ] Handle URL fetch (requires asyncronous download/extraction in backend)
- [ ] Set datatype explicitly (useful for URL fetch when filename is nonsense)
- [ ] Handle yaml description (I'm not even sure what this means, but [John mentioned it](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202))
- [ ] Handle zip archives in RO-crate spec (depends on client-side RO-crate libs)
- [ ] Allow upload to a collection (rather than dumping into history)
- [ ] Checkbox to upload into a new history
- [ ] Create an archive wizard flow, where user can explore the archive contents and selectively upload (see below)
- [ ] Accept history dataset (e.g. `tar`) as an input (there's way to extract an archive in the history currently)

**Things that we might *not* want to do**
- We don't want to upload a whole archive unless really necessary. As much client-side as possible.
- Upload multiple archives: not sure this makes sense with the "extract wizard" flow unless we use a rule-builder approach

## Features

**Datatype select**
Each datatype can be handled in multiple ways (e.g. zip can be either bagit/RO-crate spec). We probably want a `format` select component with options `None`, `BagIt`, `RO-crate` that could apply to either a zip/tar upload.

**Extract wizard**
- Blindly dumping an entire archive into the current history will often create a horrible UX
- Assume that the user might want to extract archive elements to multiple collections/histories
- Either examine and display archive content in the client, or have the backend return a dir tree that the user can explore in the frontend (the latter is probably required for URL fetch)
- The user can then checkbox-select files/dirs and choose to send them to a history as datasets or collection
- As above, but use the rule-builder

**URL fetch**
- This would be enabled implicitly if we allow history datasets as archive input.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641","id":1597556189,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5fOMXd","number":15641,"title":"Singularity jobs fail on first run waiting for image pull","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2023-02-23T21:31:45Z","updated_at":"2023-03-29T00:50:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
I'm sure this has been noticed before, but I could not find an issue for it.

When singularity-enabled tools run for the first time they fail waiting for the image pull from biocontainers, with an obscure error:

```
FATAL:   Unable to handle docker://quay.io/biocontainers/gtdbtk:2.2.2--pyhdfd78af_0 uri: unable to create tmp file: open /mnt/singularity_data/tmp/sbuild-tmp-cache-171554111: no such file or directory
```

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: usegalaxy.org.au
Commit: 22.05

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install a new tool to use the singularity runner (or delete the image for an existing one)
2. Try to run the tool for the first time 

**Expected behavior**
The tool should work first time following install or update. I couldn't locate the code that throws `unable to create tmp file` but at this point I would expect the code to wait until the image pull has completed.

tps://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/15860","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15860","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15860.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15860.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Talked with several folks who were interested in this last week so I'm sharing this basic implementation of a galactic wizard / chat assistant that I was playing with.  Not currently linked into the UI, just go to `<instance>/wizard`.

This interface could be a chat box (though responses are generally longer than one would want to see there), or whatever else.

Tinkering with the prompt (should probably be made configurable) is something folks can explore and for a real deployment we'd almost certainly want to pursue something similar to https://supabase.com/blog/chatgpt-supabase-docs to contextualize answers using the contents of the GTN, GHelp, or the hub.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/155398/228243932-d502516d-3ba7-45c4-bfed-1fca6dbc6459.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/155398/228243204-825afdbe-cccd-49b9-a3df-6a8b06c8d6d0.png)

Right now, this also (intentionally) doesn't allow follow-up questions to avoid having to track/store context, but it would be straightforward to add.

Anyway, this could go in any number of directions -- just wanted to toss it out here for folks to play with and think about.

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.

  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

is in many parts well documented. But it could be improved. 

For instance admin stuff:

- https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12272
- https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14068

Also doc strings of functions and classes. To me coding in Galaxy too often feels like guessing around and detective work. 
This is because it is mostly not documented what a function/class is supposed to do, what the parameters/return values are and how they influence the function. Tests and type hints (if available) often help a bit but should not replace docs.  Also digging out commit messages and reading discussions of PRs should not be necessary.

My suggestion would be add a checkbox analogous to the test question to the PR template in order to nudge contributors to add at least rudimentary documentation. 

We probably should also add something like https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/10348 here?





## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

I have seen a lot of Galaxy code where this is missing and I spend hours trying to understand something where a previous dev could have spent 10 minutes on a few nice docstrings.

I totally understand the concern about the overhead of keeping docs updated, but most Galaxy code doesn't require a dedicated webpage to be documented.

- docstrings are easily maintained as code changes
- they can make PRs easier to review - a diff on a docstring gives some context on the code diffs to follow
- they capture design decisions (why was the code written this way? is there some non-obvious problem that is solved here?)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/1421954773/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

is in many parts well documented. But it could be improved. 

For instance admin stuff:

- https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12272
- https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14068

Also doc strings of functions and classes. To me coding in Galaxy too often feels like guessing around and detective work. 
This is because it is mostly not documented what a function/class is supposed to do, what the parameters/return values are and how they influence the function. Tests and type hints (if available) often help a bit but should not replace docs.  Also digging out commit messages and reading discussions of PRs should not be necessary.

My suggestion would be add a checkbox analogous to the test question to the PR template in order to nudge contributors to add at least rudimentary documentation. 

We probably should also add something like https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/10348 here?





## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

This is what I would advocate for. I certainly don't think every function/class requires one. But there are many cases where I think this would be beneficial for newcomers.

I'm not sure what the best path forward is. It seems that we all agree on the intention here but there are different ideas about the implementation. Some suggested that more junior/mid level devs should:

1. Be added to PR review (to confirm that the code is legible to them)
2. Contribute more PRs improving the legibility of code (e.g. by adding some appropriate docstrings).

I think these are both great ideas. I created a list collection from a set of running (orange) history items. The collection appears as completed (green) when the items are still running. When I open the collection, the items are displayed with the correct state. When I return to the history the collection still has the incorrect (green) state.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 22.05 (Galaxy AU)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour:
1. Run a tool with multiple outputs
2. Create a collection from outputs while still running
3. Observe that the collection status is "completed"

**Expected behaviour**
I guess that the state of a collection is ambiguous since you can create a collection from datasets of mixed state. I would expect the collection state should probably be set to the "lowest" internal state (where failed is lowest and completed is highest).

the bug**
I created a list collection from a set of running (orange) history items. The collection appears as completed (green) when the items are still running. When I open the collection, the items are displayed with the correct state. When I return to the history the collection still has the incorrect (green) state.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 22.05 (Galaxy AU)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour:
1. Run a tool with multiple outputs
2. Create a collection from outputs while still running
3. Observe that the collection status is "completed"

**Expected behaviour**
I guess that the state of a collection is ambiguous since you can create a collection from datasets of mixed state. I would expect the collection state to be set to the "lowest" internal state (where failed is lowest and completed is highest).

## Existing Flow:
**Previously**, if a user tried to log in to Galaxy using OIDC, with an account that has the same email as an existing Galaxy account, we would raise an exception (in `custoz_authnz`) and show this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78516064/202600492-6a335feb-2a3a-4972-8e0a-c3133a64b00f.png)

## Changes added in this PR:
In this PR, instead of just throwing an exception, we have redirected the user so that they can log in and then connect the existing account, like so:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78516064/202600886-4c2f4611-c358-43bd-a0f6-15971d09181b.png)

which leads the user to the **User Preferences** section, where they can connect via OIDC:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/78516064/202601281-88bb13f3-e38f-487e-a8ec-8f1b13ff6fd3.png)


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)

- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.

We had some thoughts on the login order.

It would be great if this option is generic, rather than specific to custos:

```yml
# galaxy.yml
  prefer_custos_login: true  # should probably be prefer_oidc_login?
```

Perhaps an `order` tag in OIDC providers makes sense for when multiple providers are configured? This would specify vertical ordering of login options in the login/register UI.

```xml
<!-- oidc_backends_config.xml -->
    <provider name="keycloak" preferred="true">  <!-- what does preferred do? -->
        <order>1</order>  <!-- Should we add something like this? -->
        <url>https://my.idp.com/</url>
        <client_id>123-456-abcd</client_id>
        <client_secret>xyz-secret</client_secret>
        <redirect_uri>http://localhost:8080/authnz/keycloak/callback</redirect_uri>
        <well_known_oidc_config_uri>https://my-odp/.well-known/openid-configuration</well_known_oidc_config_uri>
        <icon>https://my-icon/icon.png</icon>
    </provider>
```

Happy to work on this (and Maddie's UI feedback) when we reach an agreement on the design.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/1437696931/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15641","id":1597556189,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5fOMXd","number":15641,"title":"Singularity jobs fail on first run waiting for image pull","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-02-23T21:31:45Z","updated_at":"2023-02-23T21:31:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
I'm sure this has been noticed before, but I could not find an issue for it.

When singularity-enabled tools run for the first time they fail waiting for the image pull from biocontainers, with an obscure error:

```
FATAL:   Unable to handle docker://quay.io/biocontainers/gtdbtk:2.2.2--pyhdfd78af_0 uri: unable to create tmp file: open /mnt/singularity_data/tmp/sbuild-tmp-cache-171554111: no such file or directory
```

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: usegalaxy.org.au
Commit: 22.05

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install a new tool to use the singularity runner (or delete the image for an existing one)
2. Try to run the tool for the first time 

**Expected behavior**
The tool should work first time following install or update. I couldn't locate the code that throws `unable to create tmp file` but at this point I would expect the code to wait until the image pull has completed.

URL","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-12-20T22:00:40Z","updated_at":"2023-01-03T04:06:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pulls/5025","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pull/5025","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pull/5025.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pull/5025.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Update the GTDB data manager default URL to match the current version of the tool.

GTDB-Tk versions are tightly linked to the GTDB version. The default version offered by the data manager does not match the currently released GTDB-Tk tool version.

- [x] Set default DB URL to `R202`, which matches GTDB-Tk v1.7.0 (the currently existing version)
- [x] Add some help text and link to version matching table to help admins install the appropriate DB

---

An additional comment - should the DB URL field be a `select` field, with hard-coded values? That way admins will easily be able to select the appropriate version to download, and we can hard-code the correct DB ID into the GTDB-Tk tool wrapper so that users don't need to figure out which DB is appropriate for the current tool version (I picture lots of failed jobs). Something like:

```yml
Select:
  - R207_v2: matches GTDB-Tk v2.1.0+
  - R207: matches GTDB-Tk v2.0.0
  - R202: matches GTDB-Tk v1.5.0 - v1.7.0
```

I'll create a separate issue for this if someone agrees.

{"action":"opened","number":15288,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/15288","id":1191682023,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc5HB5_n","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15288","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15288.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/15288.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15288","number":15288,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Html description","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Resolve https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15286.

This PR adds hard-coded meta `description` tags for pages that Google seems to like showing in search results.

e.g.
```html
<!-- default -->
<meta name="description" content="Galaxy is a community-driven web-based analysis platform for life science research.">
```
```html
<!-- /login/start/ -->
<meta name="description" content="Log in to Galaxy to get access to more tools and resources.">
```
```html
<!-- /workflows/ -->
<meta name="description" content="Galaxy Workflows facilitate rigorous, reproducible analysis pipelines that can be shared with the community.">
```

- The content is just a suggestion - feel free to request changes (suggested limit is ~150 chars)
- We might want to inherit these descriptions from `galaxy.config` so admins can set their own descriptions

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15286.

This PR adds hard-coded meta `description` tags for pages that Google seems to like showing in search results.

e.g.
```html
<!-- default -->
<meta name="description" content="Galaxy is a community-driven web-based analysis platform for life science research.">
```
```html
<!-- /login/start/ -->
<meta name="description" content="Log in to Galaxy to get access to more tools and resources.">
```
```html
<!-- /workflows/ -->
<meta name="description" content="Galaxy Workflows facilitate rigorous, reproducible analysis pipelines that can be shared with the community.">
```

- The content is just a suggestion - feel free to request changes (suggested limit is ~150 chars)
- We might want to inherit these descriptions from `galaxy.config` so admins can set their own descriptions

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

It seems like Galaxy expects env vars to always be strings:

```yml
  toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/maker/maker/.*:
    scheduling:
      accept:
        - pulsar
    cores: 8
    mem: 30.39
    env:
      MAKER_MPI: 1
```

On job handler logs:
```
galaxy.security.object_wrapper WARNING 2023-01-11 06:33:41,550 [pN:handler_1,p:2530827,tN:SlurmRunner.work_thread-2] Unable to create dynamic subclass SafeStringWrapper(galaxy.model.none_like.None:<class 'NoneType'>,<class 'NotImplementedType'>,<class 'bool'>,<class 'bytearray'>,<class 'ellipsis'>,<class 'galaxy.security.object_wrapper.SafeStringWrapper'>,<class 'galaxy.tools.wrappers.ToolParameterValueWrapper'>,<class 'numbers.Number'>) for <class 'galaxy.model.none_like.NoneDataset'>, None: type() doesn't support MRO entry resolution; use types.new_class()
```

In the history, the job fails with no stdout/stderr.

Changing the vortex conf to this fixes the issue:

```yml
    env:
      MAKER_MPI: "1"
```

Can TPV stringify all env vars to avoid this?

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296","id":1530079426,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5bMyjC","number":15296,"title":"Archive upload UI","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-01-12T04:29:47Z","updated_at":"2023-01-12T04:29:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This is an extension to John Chilton's https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202 so we can flesh out how the archive upload/extract will behave.

I have made a crude start on [this branch](https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy/tree/archive-uploads). There are lots of open ends and this is a long way from PR.

## Current status
- [x] Upload single tar/zip archive
- [x] Extract flattened content to the current history

The API accepts tar/zip upload requests at `/api/tools/fetch` where `target.extract_from = 'archive'`.
Can also pass `extract_from=bagit_archive` to extract an archive (tar/zip) packaged in bagit format. See [data_fetch.py](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/data_fetch.py#L95-L98).

**Bugs**
- [ ] Reset button
- [ ] Cancel button
- [ ] Genome select in table row
- [ ] Remove "Selected 1 file(s)" while `multiple="false"`
- [ ] `auto` datatype - see below

**Things that we probably want to do**
- [ ] Handle URL fetch
- [ ] Handle yaml description (I can't see where/how the backend handles this)
- [ ] Handle zip archives with RO-crate format (no backend for this)
- [ ] Allow upload to a collection (rather than dumping into history)
- [ ] Create an archive wizard flow, where user can explore the archive contents and selectively upload (see below)

**Things that we might *not* want to do**
- Upload multiple archives: not sure this makes sense with the "extract wizard" flow

## Features

**Datatype select**
I'm not sure we can reuse the existing component, since the same datatype can be handled in multiple ways (e.g. zip archive in bagit/RO-crate format). Instead we probably want a `format` select component with options `None`, `BagIt`, `RO-crate` that could apply to either a zip/tar upload.

**Extract wizard**
- Dumping an entire archive into the current history creates a horrible UX
- Assume that the user might want to extract a single archive to multiple collections/histories
- Either examine and display archive content in the client, or have the backend return a dir tree that the user can explore in the frontend
- The user can then checkbox-select files/dirs and choose to send them to a history (new/current/choose) or a collection
- As above, but use the rule-builder

{"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/72","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/72/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/72/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/72/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/total-perspective-vortex/issues/72","id":1529906893,"node_id":"I_kwDOFe5uac5bMIbN","number":72,"title":"TPV should stringify env vars","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-01-12T00:27:11Z","updated_at":"2023-01-12T05:18:11Z","closed_at":"2023-01-12T05:18:11Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This entry to `vortex_config.yml` caused a Galaxy error. It seems like Galaxy expects env vars to always be strings:

```yml
  toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/maker/maker/.*:
    scheduling:
      accept:
        - pulsar
    cores: 8
    mem: 30.39
    env:
      MAKER_MPI: 1
```

On job handler logs:
```
galaxy.security.object_wrapper WARNING 2023-01-11 06:33:41,550 [pN:handler_1,p:2530827,tN:SlurmRunner.work_thread-2] Unable to create dynamic subclass SafeStringWrapper(galaxy.model.none_like.None:<class 'NoneType'>,<class 'NotImplementedType'>,<class 'bool'>,<class 'bytearray'>,<class 'ellipsis'>,<class 'galaxy.security.object_wrapper.SafeStringWrapper'>,<class 'galaxy.tools.wrappers.ToolParameterValueWrapper'>,<class 'numbers.Number'>) for <class 'galaxy.model.none_like.NoneDataset'>, None: type() doesn't support MRO entry resolution; use types.new_class()
```

In the history, the job fails with no stdout/stderr.

Changing the vortex conf to this fixes the issue:

```yml
    env:
      MAKER_MPI: "1"
```

Can TPV stringify all env vars to avoid this?

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/15296","id":1530079426,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5bMyjC","number":15296,"title":"Archive upload UI","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-01-12T04:29:47Z","updated_at":"2023-01-13T08:23:18Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This is an extension to John Chilton's https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6202 so we can flesh out how the archive upload/extract feature will behave.

I have made a crude start on [this branch](https://github.com/neoformit/galaxy/tree/archive-uploads). There are lots of open ends and this is a long way from PR.

## Current status
- [x] Upload single tar/zip archive
- [x] Extract flattened content to the current history

The API accepts tar/zip upload requests at `/api/tools/fetch` where `target.extract_from = 'archive'`.
Can also pass `extract_from=bagit_archive` to extract an archive (tar/zip) packaged in bagit format. See [data_fetch.py](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/tools/data_fetch.py#L95-L98).

**Bugs**
- [ ] Reset button
- [ ] Cancel button
- [ ] Genome select in table row
- [ ] Remove "Selected 1 file(s)" while `multiple="false"`
- [ ] `auto` datatype - see below

**Things that we probably want to do**
- [ ] Handle URL fetch
- [ ] Set datatype explicitly (useful for URL fetch when filename is nonsense)
- [ ] Handle yaml description (I can't see where/how the backend handles this)
- [ ] Handle zip archives with RO-crate format (no backend for this)
- [ ] Allow upload to a collection (rather than dumping into history)
- [ ] Create an archive wizard flow, where user can explore the archive contents and selectively upload (see below)
- [ ] Accept inputs from history dataset (there's no existing way to extract an archive)

**Things that we might *not* want to do**
- Upload multiple archives: not sure this makes sense with the "extract wizard" flow

## Features

**Datatype select**
I'm not sure we can reuse the existing component, since the same datatype can be handled in multiple ways (e.g. zip archive in bagit/RO-crate format). Instead we probably want a `format` select component with options `None`, `BagIt`, `RO-crate` that could apply to either a zip/tar upload.

**Extract wizard**
- Dumping an entire archive into the current history creates a horrible UX
- Assume that the user might want to extract a single archive to multiple collections/histories
- Either examine and display archive content in the client, or have the backend return a dir tree that the user can explore in the frontend
- The user can then checkbox-select files/dirs and choose to send them to a history (new/current/select) or a collection
- As above, but use the rule-builder

The drag-and-drop upload overlay is triggered when trying to copy items between histories in the side-by-side view. This prevents me from completing the action. In the screenshot below, I'm trying to drop the "mutant" dataset into the history but the event is stolen by the overlay. If you drag fast enough between the histories you can avoid triggering the overlay but it's still bad UX.

I should be able to drag and drop a dataset from one history to another.

**Screenshots**

the bug**

Testing #14708.

I may have done something wrong, but I followed John's testing instructions and my upload jobs all went to the main thread. The `foo` queue didn't receive any jobs.

**To Reproduce**
1. Check out branch `release_23.0`
2. Add the following to `galaxy.yml`:
    ```yml
    celery_conf:
      task_routes:
        'galaxy.fetch_data': 'myfoo'
    ```
3. Run the following commands in two separate terminals:
    ```sh
    # Main thread
    GALAXY_CONFIG_FILE=config/galaxy.yml uvicorn --app-dir lib --factory galaxy.webapps.galaxy.fast_factory:factory
    # foo queue
    GALAXY_CONFIG_FILE=config/galaxy.yml PYTHONPATH=lib celery --app galaxy.celery worker --concurrency 2 --loglevel INFO --pool threads --queues myfoo
    ```
4. Open Galaxy and upload a file.

**Expected behavior**
The upload task is supposed to go to the `foo` queue but I found that it went to the main thread.

the bugs**
Drag-and-drop between histories mostly works (allowing for for issue #15361) but I notices a few minor issues. This is all done in side-by-side history view:

1. Drag-and-drop a collection item results in a popup "Error: request failed". I'm not sure if this is supposed to work but it would be a nice feature. Either implement or don't allow drag-drop on collection items.
1. If I start an upload and then switch to a different history, the orange "uploading" item is duplicated across two histories, and only resolves in the destination history. The duplicate history item seems to keep loading forever.
1. If I have an empty history in SBS view, I can see the "You can load your own data" button at the top of the history, but of course this uploads data to the selected history instead. So either hide this button or implement the feature.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
23.0

**Screenshots**
2\) Switched history after uploading to "Unnamed history":

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/214454370-54439c95-0110-4b04-b52b-c34e7bf08e8c.png)

3\) The UI suggests that I can upload data to an inactive history, but of course the upload goes to the selected history instead:

the bug**
Testing #14646.

I've created a collection with `txt`, `tabular` and `csv` datatypes (two items) and can't access the "convert datatype" feature on the collection.

- The collection is described as "a list with 2 datasets", instead of "a list with 2 `txt` datasets" as Assunta's PR suggests
- When I open "Edit attributes" on the collection I see "Database/build" and "Convert" tabs but not "Datatypes"

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
23.0

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a collection of a single datatype e.g. `tabular`
2. Click on pencil icon (edit attributes)
3. Confirm observations above

**Expected behaviour**
I should be able to see a "Datasets" tab where I can convert the datatype of all datasets in the collection.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/214460008-dd8dab53-c499-4902-bc92-44a8ef56ce4e.png)

the bug**
Testing #14646.

I've created a collection with `txt`, `tabular` and `csv` datatypes (two items) and can't access the "convert datatype" feature on the collection.

- The collection is described as "a list with 2 datasets", instead of "a list with 2 `txt` datasets" as Assunta's PR suggests
- When I open "Edit attributes" on the collection I see "Database/build" and "Convert" tabs but not "Datatypes"

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
23.0

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a collection of a single datatype e.g. `tabular`
2. Click on pencil icon (edit attributes)
3. Confirm observations above

**Expected behaviour**
I should be able to see a "Datasets" tab where I can convert the datatype of all datasets in the collection.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/214460008-dd8dab53-c499-4902-bc92-44a8ef56ce4e.png)

the bug**

Testing #14708.

**I may have done something wrong**, but I followed John's testing instructions and my upload jobs all went to the main thread. The `foo` queue didn't receive any tasks.

**To Reproduce**
1. Check out branch `release_23.0`
2. Add the following to `galaxy.yml`:
    ```yml
    celery_conf:
      task_routes:
        'galaxy.fetch_data': 'myfoo'
    ```
3. Run the following commands in four separate terminals:
    ```sh
    # Main thread
    GALAXY_CONFIG_FILE=config/galaxy.yml uvicorn --app-dir lib --factory galaxy.webapps.galaxy.fast_factory:factory
    # Internal queue
    GALAXY_CONFIG_FILE=config/galaxy.yml PYTHONPATH=lib celery --app galaxy.celery worker --concurrency 2 --loglevel INFO --pool threads --queues galaxy.internal
    # External queue
    GALAXY_CONFIG_FILE=config/galaxy.yml PYTHONPATH=lib celery --app galaxy.celery worker --concurrency 2 --loglevel INFO --pool threads --queues galaxy.external
    # foo queue
    GALAXY_CONFIG_FILE=config/galaxy.yml PYTHONPATH=lib celery --app galaxy.celery worker --concurrency 2 --loglevel INFO --pool threads --queues myfoo
    ```
4. Open Galaxy and upload a local file.

**Expected behavior**
The upload task is supposed to go to the `foo` queue but I found that it went to the main thread.

the bug**
Testing #14464.

The `/datatypes` page should show a list of available datatypes but on Ubuntu/Chrome I got a long table of blank rows (screenshot below). I don't see any console or request errors. Hard-refresh did not fix.

The request to https://usegalaxy.org/api/datatypes?extension_only=true appears to be successful, returning a list of datatype strings:
```js
["ab1","affybatch","agilentbrukeryep.d.tar","agilentmasshunter.d.tar", ... ]
```

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
23.0

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour:
1. Visit https://usegalaxy.org/datatypes

**Expected behaviour**
The table should be populated with available datatypes.

**Screenshots**

```
<urlopen error [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:997)>
```

Currently, we get this error on usegalaxy.org.au, but not staging.usegalaxy.org.au. I haven't been able to figure out why this has started happening on usegalaxy.org.au as the first DB install worked fine.

I have tried different URLs and changing the URL to `http`. This Python error is described in a lot of detail [here](https://askubuntu.com/questions/1401379/certificate-verify-failed-error) with a potential solution that I have implemented in [a fork](https://github.com/neoformit/tools-iuc/pull/2/files) (untested).

I hope somebody here may have seen this before in other data managers, and can advise?

GTDB-Tk versions are tightly linked to the GTDB version. The default version offered by the data manager does not match the currently released GTDB-Tk tool version.

- [x] Set default DB URL to `R202`, which matches GTDB-Tk v1.7.0 (the currently existing version)
- [x] Add some help text and link to version matching table to help admins install the appropriate DB

---

An additional comment - should the DB URL field be a `select` field, with hard-coded values? That way admins will easily be able to select the appropriate version to download, and we can hard-code the correct DB ID into the GTDB-Tk tool wrapper so that users don't need to figure out which DB is appropriate for the current tool version (I picture lots of failed jobs). Something like:

```yml
Select:
  - R207_v2: matches GTDB-Tk v2.1.0+
  - R207: matches GTDB-Tk v2.0.0
  - R202: matches GTDB-Tk v1.5.0 - v1.7.0
```

I'll create a separate issue for this if someone agrees.

---

An additional comment - should the DB URL field be a `select` field, with hard-coded values? That way admins will easily be able to select the appropriate version to download, and we can hard-code the correct DB ID into the GTDB-Tk tool wrapper so that users don't need to figure out which DB is appropriate for the current tool version (I picture lots of failed jobs). Something like:

```yml
Select:
  - R207_v2: matches GTDB-Tk v2.1.0+
  - R207: matches GTDB-Tk v2.0.0
  - R202: matches GTDB-Tk v1.5.0 - v1.7.0
```

I'll create a separate issue for this if someone agrees.

Still figuring that out. Ok, let's assume the GTDB data manager choices are hard-coded, so we know the correct DB has an ID of `R202`.

Then for the current GTDB-Tk tool we could have something like:

```xml
<options from_data_table="gtdbtk_database">
  <validator type="no_options" message="No locally cached GTDB-Tk database is available"/>
  <validator type="expression" message="GTDB R202 is required for this version of the GTDB-Tk tool. Please request support if this version is unavailable">value == "R202"</validator>
</options>
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/comments/1360513051/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

probably still needs work. It passes the tests and looks fine in the UI, but I'm not sure if all the legacy functionality has been captured.

### It can:

- Create a `input type="text"`
- Create a `input type="password"` with prop `type="password"`
- Create a `textarea` with prop `area=true`
- Create a `datalist` for the above with prop `datalist=[ 'data', 'list', 'options' ]`
- Take `multiple` input in a `textarea` with prop `multiple=true`
- Apply custom styling to the input element with prop `style={ attr: value, ... }`
- Apply custom `color` to the style object with prop `color="black"`
- Pass `id`, `value`, `readonly` and `placeholder` props onto the input element

### Questions:
- The text input takes `multiple` values using a newline-delimited values in a `textarea` - is that correct?
- The way the `input` / `textarea` switch is implemented causes it to render `<b-form-textarea>` without a closing tag. It seems to work fine, but I can't see any docs supporting the omission of the closing tag. I can change this if it looks like a concern?
- I copied the alert logic from @OlegZharkov but currently there is no validation. I could add a `required` or `nullable` prop and validate not-null. I could take a custom validate function as a prop. Or just delete this entirely?

vue.js","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This probably still needs work. It passes the tests and looks fine in the UI, but I'm not sure if all the legacy functionality has been captured.

### It can:

- Create a `input type="text"`
- Create a `input type="password"` with prop `type="password"`
- Create a `textarea` with prop `area=true`
- Create a `datalist` for the above with prop `datalist=[ 'data', 'list', 'options' ]`
- Take `multiple` input in a `textarea` with prop `multiple=true`
- Apply custom styling to the input element with prop `style={ attr: value, ... }`
- Apply custom `color` to the style object with prop `color="black"`
- Pass `id`, `value`, `readonly` and `placeholder` props onto the input element

### Questions:
- The text input takes `multiple` values using a newline-delimited values in a `textarea` - is that correct?
- The way the `input` / `textarea` switch is implemented causes it to render `<b-form-textarea>` without a closing tag. It seems to work fine, but I can't see any docs supporting the omission of the closing tag. I can change this if it looks like a concern?
- I copied the alert logic from @OlegZharkov but currently there is no validation. I could add a `required` or `nullable` prop and validate not-null. I could take a custom validate function as a prop. Or just delete this entirely?

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).

probably still needs work. It passes the tests and looks fine in the UI, but I'm not sure if all the legacy functionality has been captured.

### It can:

- Create a `input type="text"`
- Create a `input type="password"` with prop `type="password"`
- Create a `textarea` with prop `area=true`
- Create a `datalist` for the above with prop `datalist=[ 'data', 'list', 'options' ]`
- Take `multiple` input in a `textarea` with prop `multiple=true`
- Apply custom styling to the input element with prop `style={ attr: value, ... }`
- Apply custom `color` to the style object with prop `color="black"`
- Pass `id`, `value`, `readonly` and `placeholder` props onto the input element

### Questions:
- The text input takes `multiple` values using a newline-delimited values in a `textarea` - is that correct?
- The way the `input` / `textarea` switch is implemented causes it to render `<b-form-textarea>` without a closing tag. It seems to work fine, but I can't see any docs supporting the omission of the closing tag. I can change this if it looks like a concern?
- I copied the alert logic from @OlegZharkov but currently there is no validation. I could add a `required` or `nullable` prop and validate not-null. I could take a custom validate function as a prop. Or just delete this entirely?

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.

  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

probably still needs work. It passes the tests and looks fine in the UI, but I'm not sure if all the legacy functionality has been captured.

### It can:

- Create a `input type="text"`
- Create a `input type="password"` with prop `type="password"`
- Create a `textarea` with prop `area=true`
- Create a `datalist` for the above with prop `datalist=[ 'data', 'list', 'options' ]`
- Take `multiple` input in a `textarea` with prop `multiple=true`
- Apply custom styling to the input element with prop `style={ attr: value, ... }`
- Apply custom `color` to the style object with prop `color="black"`
- Pass `id`, `value`, `readonly` and `placeholder` props onto the input element

### Questions:
- The text input takes `multiple` values using a newline-delimited values in a `textarea` - is that correct?
- The way the `input` / `textarea` switch is implemented causes it to render `<b-form-textarea>` without a closing tag. It seems to work fine, but I can't see any docs supporting the omission of the closing tag. I can change this if it looks like a concern?
- I copied the alert logic from @OlegZharkov but currently there is no validation. I could add a `required` or `nullable` prop and validate not-null. I could take a custom validate function as a prop. Or just delete this entirely?

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [x] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

,"title":"Dev email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

,"title":"Dev email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

+"""Test custom template configuration and rendering."""
+
+from galaxy.util.custom_templates import template
+
+TEMPLATE_RELPATH = "mail/activation-email.txt"
+CONTEXT = {
+    "name": "Jane Doe",
+    "user_email": "jane@example.com",
+    "date": "01-01-2021",
+    "hostname": "example.galaxy.org",
+    "activation_url": "https://example.galaxy.org/fake-activation-link",
+    "terms_url": "https://example.galaxy.org/terms",
+    "contact_email": "contact@example.galaxy.org",
+    "instance_resource_url": "https://example.galaxy.org/resources",
+    "expiry_days": 7,
+}
+CUSTOM_TEMPLATE = """
+Here's my custom template. It starts with a comment section to let admins
+know how to use it.
+
+>>>>>> The actual template starts from the next line
+This is my custom template!
+
+Name: {{ name }}
+
+"""
+DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_HEADER_SUBSTRING = "The following variables are available"
+DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_OUTPUT_SUBSTRING = f"Hello {CONTEXT['name']}"
+CUSTOM_TEMPLATE_OUTPUT_SUBSTRING = f"Name: {CONTEXT['name']}"
+
+
+def test_it_can_render_a_default_template(tmp_path):
+    """It should fall back on codebase default."""
+    custom_templates_dir = tmp_path
+    output = template.render(
+        TEMPLATE_RELPATH,
+        CONTEXT,
+        custom_templates_dir,
+    )
+    # Make sure it's parsed out the doc header
+    assert DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_HEADER_SUBSTRING not in output
+    # Make sure the template content is correct
+    assert DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_OUTPUT_SUBSTRING in output
+
+
+def test_it_can_render_a_custom_template(tmp_path):
+    """It should use the custom template and ignore the default."""
+    custom_templates_dir = tmp_path

+    template_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

+        f.write(CUSTOM_TEMPLATE)
+    # It should find the custom template
+    output = template.render(
+        TEMPLATE_RELPATH,
+        CONTEXT,
+        custom_templates_dir,
+    )

email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

,"title":"Dev html email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

+"""Test custom template configuration and rendering."""
+
+from galaxy.util.custom_templates import template

+    "terms_url": "https://example.galaxy.org/terms",
+    "contact_email": "contact@example.galaxy.org",

+
+>>>>>> The actual template starts from the next line
+This is my custom template!
+
+Name: {{ name }}
+
+"""
+DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_HEADER_SUBSTRING = "The following variables are available"
+DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_OUTPUT_SUBSTRING = f"Hello {CONTEXT['name']}"
+CUSTOM_TEMPLATE_OUTPUT_SUBSTRING = f"Name: {CONTEXT['name']}"
+
+

+        CONTEXT,

+    )

email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default

,"title":"Dev html email","user":{"login":"neoformit","id":42562517,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYyNTE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42562517?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit","html_url":"https://github.com/neoformit","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/neoformit/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Galaxy AU would like to be able to send a nice HTML body with the account activation email, that can be customised. It's the first time that we interact with new users and is a great opportunity to help get them involved!

This PR exchanges the (mostly) hard-coded text email with a templated text+html one. Existing Galaxies should be able to start using this with default templates without any config changes:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42562517/196854321-1bd9d1bb-2245-4504-be57-c0095ed87225.png)

Admins can also define a `templates_dir` in `galaxy.yml` (resolved with respect to `config_dir`) and put custom templates in there to override the default templates. This way Galaxy owners can send any content, with any style/images they like. I figured there are likely other areas where this would be useful, so I made `lib/galaxy/util/custom_templates/templates.py:render` fairly generic, so it will accept any template path and context to render. Most likely this would be used for emails.

## How to test the changes?

See `test/unit/util/test_custom_templates.py`.

- [x] Test default template renders correctly (remove doc header)
- [x] Test default templates, no customisation
- [x] Test custom template overrides default
